# Nitro

Nitro is a full-stack server framework that extends Vite applications with a production-ready server, designed to run anywhere. It provides filesystem-based routing, automatic code-splitting, built-in support for storage, caching, and databases — all runtime-agnostic and deployable to Node.js, Bun, Deno, Cloudflare Workers, Vercel, Netlify, and many more platforms without configuration changes.

At its core, Nitro compiles routes at build time, eliminating the need for a runtime router. Only the code required for each request is loaded and executed, making it ideal for serverless deployments with near-0ms boot time. Nitro seamlessly integrates with Vite as a plugin, enabling developers to add API routes, server-side rendering, and full production server capabilities to any Vite project with minimal setup.

## Installation and Setup

Install Nitro and Vite, then add the Nitro plugin to your Vite configuration to enable server-side capabilities in your project.

```bash
# Using npm
npm install nitro vite

# Using pnpm
pnpm add nitro vite

# Using yarn
yarn add nitro vite
```

```typescript
// vite.config.ts
import { defineConfig } from "vite";
import { nitro } from "nitro/vite";

export default defineConfig({
  plugins: [nitro()],
});
```

```typescript
// nitro.config.ts
import { defineConfig } from "nitro";

export default defineConfig({
  serverDir: "./server",
});
```

## defineHandler - Create Route Handlers

The `defineHandler` function creates request handlers for API routes. Handlers receive an H3 event object and can return strings, JSON objects, Response instances, or readable streams.

```typescript
// server/api/hello.ts
import { defineHandler } from "nitro";

export default defineHandler((event) => {
  return { message: "Hello from Nitro!", timestamp: Date.now() };
});

// GET /api/hello -> { "message": "Hello from Nitro!", "timestamp": 1699900000000 }
```

## Dynamic Route Parameters

Use bracket notation `[param]` in filenames to create dynamic routes. Parameters are accessible via `event.context.params`.

```typescript
// server/api/users/[id].ts
import { defineHandler } from "nitro";

export default defineHandler((event) => {
  const { id } = event.context.params;
  return { userId: id, profile: `User profile for ${id}` };
});

// GET /api/users/123 -> { "userId": "123", "profile": "User profile for 123" }
```

## Catch-All Routes

Use `[...param]` syntax to capture all remaining path segments, including slashes.

```typescript
// server/api/files/[...path].ts
import { defineHandler } from "nitro";

export default defineHandler((event) => {
  const { path } = event.context.params;
  return { filePath: path, segments: path.split("/") };
});

// GET /api/files/docs/images/logo.png
// -> { "filePath": "docs/images/logo.png", "segments": ["docs", "images", "logo.png"] }
```

## HTTP Method-Specific Routes

Append HTTP method to filename to restrict route to specific methods.

```typescript
// server/api/posts.get.ts
import { defineHandler } from "nitro";

export default defineHandler(() => {
  return [{ id: 1, title: "First Post" }, { id: 2, title: "Second Post" }];
});

// server/api/posts.post.ts
import { defineHandler } from "nitro";

export default defineHandler(async (event) => {
  const body = await event.req.json();
  return { created: true, post: { id: 3, ...body } };
});

// GET /api/posts -> [{ "id": 1, "title": "First Post" }, ...]
// POST /api/posts with body { "title": "New Post" } -> { "created": true, "post": { "id": 3, "title": "New Post" } }
```

## defineMiddleware - Create Middleware

Middleware run before route handlers and can modify the request context. They should not return a value (returning terminates the request).

```typescript
// server/middleware/auth.ts
import { defineMiddleware } from "nitro";

export default defineMiddleware((event) => {
  const token = event.req.headers.get("authorization");
  event.context.user = token ? { authenticated: true, token } : null;
});

// server/api/profile.ts
import { defineHandler } from "nitro";

export default defineHandler((event) => {
  if (!event.context.user) {
    throw new Error("Unauthorized");
  }
  return { user: event.context.user };
});
```

## defineCachedHandler - Cache Route Responses

Cache handler responses with configurable TTL and stale-while-revalidate behavior.

```typescript
// server/api/weather.ts
import { defineCachedHandler } from "nitro/cache";

export default defineCachedHandler(
  async () => {
    const response = await fetch("https://api.weather.example/current");
    const data = await response.json();
    return { weather: data, cachedAt: new Date().toISOString() };
  },
  {
    maxAge: 60 * 5, // Cache for 5 minutes
    swr: true, // Serve stale while revalidating
    shouldBypassCache: ({ req }) => req.url.includes("refresh=true"),
  }
);

// GET /api/weather -> cached response
// GET /api/weather?refresh=true -> fresh response, bypasses cache
```

## defineCachedFunction - Cache Function Results

Cache the results of any function for reuse across multiple handlers.

```typescript
// server/utils/github.ts
import { defineCachedFunction } from "nitro/cache";

export const getGitHubStars = defineCachedFunction(
  async (repo: string) => {
    const response = await fetch(`https://api.github.com/repos/${repo}`);
    const data = await response.json();
    return data.stargazers_count;
  },
  {
    maxAge: 60 * 60, // Cache for 1 hour
    name: "githubStars",
    getKey: (repo: string) => repo,
  }
);

// server/api/stars/[...repo].ts
import { defineHandler } from "nitro";
import { getGitHubStars } from "../utils/github";

export default defineHandler(async (event) => {
  const { repo } = event.context.params;
  const stars = await getGitHubStars(repo);
  return { repo, stars };
});

// GET /api/stars/nitrojs/nitro -> { "repo": "nitrojs/nitro", "stars": 5000 }
```

## useStorage - Key-Value Storage

Access Nitro's built-in key-value storage layer powered by unstorage with support for multiple drivers.

```typescript
// server/api/settings.ts
import { defineHandler } from "nitro";
import { useStorage } from "nitro/storage";

export default defineHandler(async (event) => {
  const storage = useStorage("settings");

  if (event.method === "GET") {
    const theme = await storage.getItem("theme");
    const language = await storage.getItem("language");
    return { theme: theme || "light", language: language || "en" };
  }

  if (event.method === "POST") {
    const body = await event.req.json();
    await storage.setItem("theme", body.theme);
    await storage.setItem("language", body.language);
    return { saved: true };
  }
});

// GET /api/settings -> { "theme": "light", "language": "en" }
// POST /api/settings with { "theme": "dark", "language": "es" } -> { "saved": true }
```

## Storage Configuration

Configure storage drivers for production use including Redis, filesystem, and cloud storage.

```typescript
// nitro.config.ts
import { defineConfig } from "nitro";

export default defineConfig({
  storage: {
    cache: {
      driver: "redis",
      url: "redis://localhost:6379",
    },
    data: {
      driver: "fs",
      base: "./.data/storage",
    },
  },
  devStorage: {
    cache: {
      driver: "fs",
      base: "./.data/cache",
    },
  },
});
```

## useDatabase - SQL Database Access

Access Nitro's built-in SQL database layer with support for SQLite, PostgreSQL, MySQL, and more.

```typescript
// nitro.config.ts
import { defineConfig } from "nitro";

export default defineConfig({
  experimental: {
    database: true,
  },
});

// server/api/users.ts
import { defineHandler } from "nitro";
import { useDatabase } from "nitro/database";

export default defineHandler(async (event) => {
  const db = useDatabase();

  if (event.method === "GET") {
    const { rows } = await db.sql`SELECT * FROM users LIMIT 10`;
    return { users: rows };
  }

  if (event.method === "POST") {
    const body = await event.req.json();
    const id = crypto.randomUUID();
    await db.sql`INSERT INTO users (id, name, email) VALUES (${id}, ${body.name}, ${body.email})`;
    return { created: true, id };
  }
});

// GET /api/users -> { "users": [{ "id": "...", "name": "John", "email": "john@example.com" }] }
// POST /api/users with { "name": "Jane", "email": "jane@example.com" } -> { "created": true, "id": "..." }
```

## defineWebSocketHandler - WebSocket Support

Create real-time WebSocket handlers with pub/sub messaging support.

```typescript
// nitro.config.ts
import { defineConfig } from "nitro";

export default defineConfig({
  features: {
    websocket: true,
  },
});

// server/routes/_ws.ts
import { defineWebSocketHandler } from "nitro";

export default defineWebSocketHandler({
  open(peer) {
    peer.send({ type: "welcome", message: `Welcome ${peer.id}!` });
    peer.subscribe("chat");
    peer.publish("chat", { type: "join", user: peer.id });
  },
  message(peer, message) {
    const data = message.json();
    if (data.type === "chat") {
      peer.publish("chat", { type: "message", user: peer.id, text: data.text });
      peer.send({ type: "ack", messageId: data.id });
    }
  },
  close(peer) {
    peer.publish("chat", { type: "leave", user: peer.id });
  },
});

// Client-side connection:
// const ws = new WebSocket("ws://localhost:3000/_ws");
// ws.send(JSON.stringify({ type: "chat", text: "Hello!", id: 1 }));
```

## Server-Sent Events (SSE)

Stream real-time updates to clients using Server-Sent Events.

```typescript
// server/api/events.ts
import { defineHandler } from "nitro";
import { createEventStream } from "nitro/h3";

export default defineHandler((event) => {
  const stream = createEventStream(event);
  let count = 0;

  const interval = setInterval(async () => {
    await stream.push({
      id: String(count++),
      event: "tick",
      data: JSON.stringify({ time: new Date().toISOString(), count }),
    });
  }, 1000);

  stream.onClosed(() => {
    clearInterval(interval);
  });

  return stream.send();
});

// Client-side:
// const source = new EventSource("/api/events");
// source.addEventListener("tick", (e) => console.log(JSON.parse(e.data)));
```

## definePlugin - Server Plugins

Create plugins that run during server startup to extend Nitro's runtime behavior.

```typescript
// server/plugins/logger.ts
import { definePlugin } from "nitro";

export default definePlugin((nitroApp) => {
  nitroApp.hooks.hook("request", (event) => {
    console.log(`[${new Date().toISOString()}] ${event.req.method} ${event.req.url}`);
  });

  nitroApp.hooks.hook("response", (res, event) => {
    console.log(`[${new Date().toISOString()}] Response ${res.status} for ${event.req.url}`);
  });

  nitroApp.hooks.hook("error", (error, { event, tags }) => {
    console.error(`[ERROR] ${tags?.join(", ")}: ${error.message}`, event?.req.url);
  });

  nitroApp.hooks.hook("close", () => {
    console.log("Server shutting down...");
  });
});
```

## useRuntimeConfig - Runtime Configuration

Access environment-specific configuration that can be overridden via environment variables.

```typescript
// nitro.config.ts
import { defineConfig } from "nitro";

export default defineConfig({
  runtimeConfig: {
    apiKey: "dev_key",
    database: {
      host: "localhost",
      port: 5432,
    },
  },
});

// server/api/config.ts
import { defineHandler } from "nitro";
import { useRuntimeConfig } from "nitro/runtime-config";

export default defineHandler(() => {
  const config = useRuntimeConfig();
  return {
    hasApiKey: !!config.apiKey,
    dbHost: config.database.host,
  };
});

// Override via environment variables:
// NITRO_API_KEY="prod_key" NITRO_DATABASE_HOST="db.example.com" node .output/server/index.mjs
```

## Route Rules Configuration

Configure route-level caching, redirects, proxying, CORS, and basic auth.

```typescript
// nitro.config.ts
import { defineConfig } from "nitro";

export default defineConfig({
  routeRules: {
    // Enable SWR caching for blog routes
    "/blog/**": { swr: 3600 },

    // Add CORS headers for API
    "/api/**": { cors: true, headers: { "access-control-allow-methods": "GET,POST" } },

    // Redirect old URLs
    "/old-page": { redirect: "/new-page" },
    "/legacy/**": { redirect: { to: "/modern/**", status: 308 } },

    // Proxy requests to external service
    "/external/**": { proxy: "https://api.example.com/**" },

    // Basic auth for admin area
    "/admin/**": { basicAuth: { username: "admin", password: "secret" } },

    // Disable caching for real-time endpoints
    "/api/realtime/**": { cache: false },
  },
});
```

## Server Entry with Framework Integration

Use server entry to integrate with web frameworks like Hono, Elysia, or H3.

```typescript
// server.ts - Using Hono
import { Hono } from "hono";

const app = new Hono();

app.get("/", (c) => c.text("Hello from Hono with Nitro!"));
app.get("/json", (c) => c.json({ framework: "hono", status: "ok" }));

export default app;
```

```typescript
// server.ts - Using plain fetch handler
export default {
  fetch(req: Request) {
    const url = new URL(req.url);

    if (url.pathname === "/health") {
      return new Response("OK", { status: 200 });
    }

    // Return nothing to continue to route handlers
  },
};
```

## Tasks - Background Operations

Define and run background tasks with scheduling support.

```typescript
// nitro.config.ts
import { defineConfig } from "nitro";

export default defineConfig({
  experimental: {
    tasks: true,
  },
  scheduledTasks: {
    "*/5 * * * *": "cleanup:expired", // Run every 5 minutes
    "0 0 * * *": "reports:daily",     // Run daily at midnight
  },
});

// server/tasks/cleanup/expired.ts
export default defineTask({
  meta: {
    name: "cleanup:expired",
    description: "Remove expired sessions from database",
  },
  async run({ payload, context }) {
    const db = useDatabase();
    const { rows } = await db.sql`DELETE FROM sessions WHERE expires_at < NOW() RETURNING id`;
    return { result: { deleted: rows.length } };
  },
});

// Programmatic execution from a route:
// server/api/admin/cleanup.ts
import { defineHandler } from "nitro";
import { runTask } from "nitro/task";

export default defineHandler(async () => {
  const { result } = await runTask("cleanup:expired");
  return result;
});
```

## HTML Renderer with Template Processing

Serve dynamic HTML using Nitro's built-in template processor.

```html
<!-- index.html -->
<!DOCTYPE html>
<html lang="en">
<head>
  <title>{{ $URL.pathname }} - My App</title>
</head>
<body>
  <h1>Welcome!</h1>
  <p>Current path: {{ $URL.pathname }}</p>
  <p>Method: {{ $METHOD }}</p>

  <? if ($METHOD === 'POST') { ?>
    <script server>
      const formData = await $REQUEST.formData();
      const name = formData.get('name');
    </script>
    <p>Hello, {{ name }}!</p>
  <? } else { ?>
    <form method="POST">
      <input name="name" placeholder="Your name" />
      <button type="submit">Submit</button>
    </form>
  <? } ?>
</body>
</html>
```

```typescript
// nitro.config.ts
import { defineConfig } from "nitro";

export default defineConfig({
  renderer: {
    template: "./index.html",
  },
});
```

## Deployment Presets

Deploy to various platforms with zero configuration using built-in presets.

```typescript
// nitro.config.ts
import { defineConfig } from "nitro";

export default defineConfig({
  // Automatically detected, or set explicitly:
  // preset: "cloudflare_pages",
  // preset: "vercel",
  // preset: "netlify",
  // preset: "node_server",
});

// Build commands:
// NITRO_PRESET=cloudflare_pages npx nitro build
// NITRO_PRESET=vercel npx nitro build
// NITRO_PRESET=node_server npx nitro build
```

Nitro serves as an excellent foundation for building full-stack applications, APIs, and meta-frameworks. Its primary use cases include creating REST APIs with file-based routing, building real-time applications with WebSocket support, implementing caching strategies for performance optimization, and developing universal JavaScript applications that deploy anywhere. Popular frameworks like Nuxt, SolidStart, and TanStack Start leverage Nitro as their server foundation.

Integration patterns typically involve adding Nitro as a Vite plugin for frontend projects needing backend capabilities, using the storage and database layers for data persistence, implementing middleware for authentication and logging, and configuring route rules for caching and proxying. The framework's runtime-agnostic design means the same codebase runs on Node.js during development and deploys to edge platforms like Cloudflare Workers or Vercel Edge Functions in production without code changes.
